title: What Product Do I Use For The Depressor Angularis Oris (DAO)？
filename: What Product Do I Use For The Depressor Angularis Oris (DAO)？.en.vtt
excerpt: Hi guys I am attempting a screen share instead of a normal video so I thought I'd show you this and someone asked a question around whether you should use and will basically Botox on the lower face and particularly the depressor anguli oris and what that means for in terms of sid…

segments.text: hi guys I am attempting a screen share instead of a normal video so I thought I'd show you this and someone asked a question around whether you should use and will basically Botox on the lower face and particularly the depressor anguli oris and what that means for in terms of side effects and risks and the point I wanted to make is around dermal fillers as well because actually you can treat a downturn mouth using dermal filler very effectively and part of the effect is the effect that it has on the depressor angular asaurus so let's first just have a look at it ask me down here so it when you if you don't advanced training you'll know that we're injecting the depressor angular asaurus can be a little bit tricky so as in if you get it in the wrong place you can see by looking at this diagram where the problem might be and if you inject a little bit too deep a little bit too medial and you can get the depressor labia muscle here and if you get the

depressor labia i'm muscle a smile that and where you you're actually if i'm just drawing on top of here what a smile would look like you knock out the bit of the muscle that pulls the pulls this way and you get a smile that and let me just I'll draw it next to here actually so you'll smile that should look like this ends up looking like that and that's a very unpleasant and this is the lower lip by the way I hope this is a probably should have done a better diagram but and it's that normally this muscle pulls down and you've affected it so an asymmetrical smile is really noticeable your smile is very important to people and if you create a and a smile that doesn't look and I just realized you see me on here yeah and if you create a smile that looks asymmetrical they will notice even a little bit it's really upsetting so asymmetrical smiles are one of the problems of treating the depressor angular asaurus with toxin now in order

to avoid it it's worth noticing that there is a very clear angle triangle shape here where there's no muscle and if you contract your own DAO which you can do just by fitting around your face you'll feel that there's a little triangle that you can feel between the Rosario so depressor anguli oris and the master and that should hope you've helped you find the border of this muscle now the lower down you get to along kids the closer you get to the angle of the jaw the more likely you're going to be involved with the depressor lady eye so it's one of the reasons why and on our advanced training we'd we would teach you to inject at this midpoint by the way this is just a kind of interesting video on it I don't recommend that you treat anyone based on this video you need to actually look at a patient with it with someone who's experienced you can show you exactly and where the point is to inject but essentially this is that this is the key concept that you need to know about how to feel for it is that there's this little triangle and you can inject and the muscle in a point

roughly over here that's less likely to affect the depressor ladyís so that's the Botox side of things and I will say a couple things about it firstly Botox is and I feel like it's a little bit less predictable than the dermal fillers and a little bit less adjustable because you have to wait two weeks so you put some product in and you hope you've hit them hit the mark and then you have to wait and sometimes there's some asymmetry and sometimes it works do well and sometimes it doesn't work too well enough and that and then you're in this process of trying to make adjustments every two weeks so that's a big downside with Botox the upside is cheap because it's only a couple units at a time and so that's better for your patient but if you're gonna use a dermal filler you can actually in older people create a very similar effect which is and if you think about what happens with muscles as you get older if they aren't supported underneath and that happens because number one the bone itself gets smaller so your actual bone will get smaller as you get older which means this muscle is actually able to contract more because it's less resistant it's less stretched out that's one of the reasons and

there's more activity in older faces but also you lose the fat both on top of it and there'll be a little bit of fat underneath it and that means this muscle is able to contract more easily you get a degree of hyper dynamic and activity in muscles as you get older and that's why you get a downturn and in some people's faces obviously that's not the only reason why because underneath you've also got these fat pads which are changing so it's not to ignore you can't ever solve a problem in the face just with one means so think about what's happening with all these fat pads in faces as you get older because and this these fat pads here the gel fat pad and the lateral cheap fat pad gets they get disproportionate so naturally they often get smaller the middle cheek gets bigger the gel fat while the nasolabial fold might get bigger so this tends to get heavier and it then is pushing down on this area where the DAO is also pulling down so sometimes a way to treat it is

to replace the fat in this area and some with dermal filler and sometimes it really helps to actually affect the bone by going deep and getting underneath the muscle replacing the volume underneath the muscle and that can create an instant lifting effect which is worth thinking about so it's not as simple as just Botox or filler there are many reasons why we get older muscles get more active because they are less opposed by fat and bone gets smaller which means the muscle is able to contract more and because as leaders basically it's a long muscle honor that used to be stretched out by bone and fat and now it's more it's more free to move so that's one of the reasons why Botox is a good thing to do as well so combine them both in some people I think don't feel is more predictable I like using fellow you can create an instant and gratifying result straightaway but it's important to have it in your tool kit and to know how to avoid about the side effects and what else can I tell you let's have a

look any and any questions so I've got a few girls saying hi everyone hi Molly Jodi Rachel and I'm just attempting the screen sharing I think I prefer being on to see myself it just feels a bit and I can't switch back unfortunately so um I might look at some software to do that a different way but I hope that's helped you think a little bit about the DAO and how you can avoid an asymmetrical smile and hope it's also allowed you to think more about how filler enables you to do the same thing you can create that lifting and I'm hopefully going to get back into the rhythm of lives it's been I had the busiest last few weeks of August and September ever and I just got out of the rhythm but I'll be back into it soon doing more live videos so keep the questions coming and I will keep trying to help you guys out and but I hope that helps in the meantime please tag anyone who you think might be interested in this and thank you once again for the comments I mean a lot to me no I do read them all and thank you for watching and I'll see you
